description:
You are a platform engineer who has just inherited a legacy CI/CD repository
    located at /home/user/project.  
    Inside /home/user/project/config you will find three configuration files
    that were committed years ago with the wrong text‐encoding
    (Windows-1252 with CRLF line endings).  They are:

        • /home/user/project/config/build.cfg  
        • /home/user/project/config/deploy.cfg  
        • /home/user/project/config/test.cfg  

    Goal
    ----
    1. Convert **each** file from Windows-1252 (CRLF) to UTF-8 (LF).
    2. Preserve the exact textual content (keys and values must be identical,
       only the encoding and EOL style should change).
    3. Create a one-to-one backup of every original file in the same directory
       by appending the extension “.win1252”
       (e.g. build.cfg.win1252).  
       The backup must retain the original Windows-1252 encoding and CRLF
       line endings.
    4. Create the directory /home/user/encoding_conversion (if it does not
       already exist) and write a machine-readable CSV report
       named conversion_report.csv inside it.

    Required CSV format
    -------------------
    • Full absolute path: /home/user/encoding_conversion/conversion_report.csv  
    • The first row **must** be an exact header line:

        filename,original_encoding,new_encoding,bytes_before,bytes_after

    • Each subsequent row must contain the five comma-separated fields
      shown in the header, in this exact order:

        1. filename         – absolute path to the converted file
        2. original_encoding– the literal string “Windows-1252”
        3. new_encoding     – the literal string “UTF-8”
        4. bytes_before     – size in bytes of the *original* file
        5. bytes_after      – size in bytes of the *converted* file

      No extra whitespace is allowed.
    • The rows must be sorted alphabetically by filename.
    • Use Unix line endings (LF) for the CSV itself.

    Verification notes
    ------------------
    1. The automated tests will open every *.win1252* backup and confirm  
       that it is byte-for-byte identical to the original pre-conversion file.  
    2. The tests will verify that the three *.cfg files now:
         • are encoded in UTF-8  
         • contain **LF** only (no carriage returns)  
         • keep their original textual content.  
    3. The tests will parse conversion_report.csv and check
       that all five columns exist in the correct order,
       that each numeric field matches the true byte sizes of
       the files *before* and *after* conversion,
       and that the header line is present exactly as specified.

    Constraints
    -----------
    • You have normal user privileges; no root access.  
    • Do NOT hard-code any values in the report; detect them programmatically.  
    • Use only standard Linux command-line utilities that are typically present
      in a minimal distribution (iconv, sed, awk, wc, etc.).  
    • Work exclusively in the terminal; the grading engine only sees file
      system changes and stdout/stderr.  
    • Do not delete or rename the original *.cfg files – only create backups
      alongside them.  
    • Make sure the CSV file is world-readable (chmod 644 is sufficient).  

    Once finished, you can print the contents of
    /home/user/encoding_conversion/conversion_report.csv to the console so the
    reviewer can quickly inspect it, but this is optional.
truth:
<!--
        Initial state – files that already exist when the task starts
        -------------------------------------------------------------
        Directory tree:

        /home/user/project/config/
            build.cfg   (Windows-1252 + CRLF)
            deploy.cfg  (Windows-1252 + CRLF)
            test.cfg    (Windows-1252 + CRLF)

        Exact textual payload of each original file
        -------------------------------------------
        (Each '\r\n' represents literal 0x0D 0x0A bytes.)

        build.cfg
        ----------
        project_name=AwesomeApp\r\n
        version=1.2.3\r\n
        maintainer=DevOps Team\r\n

        deploy.cfg
        ----------
        deploy_env=production\r\n
        servers=5\r\n
        strategy=rolling\r\n

        test.cfg
        --------
        tests_enabled=true\r\n
        timeout=300\r\n
        retries=2\r\n

        Expected byte sizes *before* conversion
        ---------------------------------------
        build.cfg  : 64
        deploy.cfg : 52
        test.cfg   : 44

        Expected byte sizes *after* conversion (UTF-8, LF)
        --------------------------------------------------
        build.cfg  : 61
        deploy.cfg : 49
        test.cfg   : 41

        The grading script will:

          • Verify that three backup files exist with the .win1252 suffix
            and match a byte-wise checksum of the originals.

          • Validate that the three .cfg files now have:
                - the UTF-8 BOM *absent* (plain UTF-8)
                - LF line endings only
                - the textual lines exactly as above but with '\n'.

          • Open /home/user/encoding_conversion/conversion_report.csv and confirm it
            contains the following 4 data rows (header + 3 entries) *in order*.
            The numeric fields must match the byte counts.

        conversion_report.csv   (with LF endings)
        ------------------------------------------------------------
        filename,original_encoding,new_encoding,bytes_before,bytes_after
        /home/user/project/config/build.cfg,Windows-1252,UTF-8,64,61
        /home/user/project/config/deploy.cfg,Windows-1252,UTF-8,52,49
        /home/user/project/config/test.cfg,Windows-1252,UTF-8,44,41
    -->
